  <entry>
    <id>http://arxiv.org/abs/2301.01113v2</id>
    <title>Invalidator: Automated Patch Correctness Assessment via Semantic and Syntactic Reasoning</title>
    <updated>2023-03-17T10:56:58Z</updated>
    <link href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.01113v2" rel="alternate" type="text/html"/>
    <link href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2301.01113v2" rel="related" type="application/pdf" title="pdf"/>
    <summary>Automated program repair (APR) faces the challenge of test overfitting, where generated patches pass validation tests but fail to generalize. Existing methods for patch assessment involve generating new tests or manual inspection, which can be time-consuming or biased. In this paper, we propose a novel technique, INVALIDATOR, to automatically assess the correctness of APR-generated patches via semantic and syntactic reasoning. INVALIDATOR leverages program invariants to reason about program semantics while also capturing program syntax through language semantics learned from a large code corpus using a pre-trained language model. Given a buggy program and the developer-patched program, INVALIDATOR infers likely invariants on both programs. Then, INVALIDATOR determines that an APR-generated patch overfits if: (1) it violates correct specifications or (2) mainta
